title: 21cm Cosmology Reionization Signal
source_data_quality_note: Source MD is a widefield-fluorescence fan-out artifact; principle grounded in domain literature.

E.description: 21cm Cosmology Reionization Signal: 21cm reionization mapping: detect 21cm signal from neutral hydrogen during EoR against bright foreground emission. The forward operator produces the measurement through a 3-node primitive DAG (F.fourier.power_spectrum_3d…); recovery is posed as a statistical_inverse problem. Difficulty tier delta=10 with effective condition number kappa_eff~5000; calibration_errors_percent, ionospheric_distortion set the accuracy floor at the Omega boundary. See the forward_model field for the closed-form equation.
E.forward_model: T_b(z) = 27*x_HI*(1+delta)*((1+z)/10) ** 0.5 * (H/dv_r/dr) mK; foregrounds dominate at 4 orders of magnitude
E.world_state_x: HI neutral fraction x_HI(z) and 21cm brightness temperature power spectrum P_21cm(k,z)
E.observation_y: calibrated visibility data V(u,v,nu) from radio interferometer (HERA/SKA-Low)

W.regime: Existence of the recovered 3D_21cm_brightness_temperature is guaranteed within the declared Omega bounds. Uniqueness holds on the measurement-supported subspace; out-of-support modes are controlled by declared priors. Stability is conditionally stable (kappa_eff ~= 5000); calibration_errors_percent dominates the stability cliff; the remaining mismatch parameters contribute higher-order bias terms. Thermal gaussian sets the irreducible data-fidelity floor.

C.error_bound: Data-fidelity bound: 2-sigma detection at k ~ 0.1 h/Mpc requires 1000h HERA integration. Noise floor: O(sigma / sqrt(kappa_eff)) with kappa_eff~5000. Mismatch-induced bias + C2 * |calibration_errors_percent|; iterative solvers achieve q=2 convergence (O(1/k^2)) near the optimum; epsilon_fn captures quality degradation across declared Omega.
C.complexity: O(N_baselines * N_freq * N_time) for calibration + imaging per iteration
